title: nepeta teucriifolia subsp. carmanica
intro: Nepeta teucriifolia subsp. carmanica, commonly known as Carman's Catmint, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to the mountains of western Turkey. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Nepeta teucriifolia subsp. carmanica include relief from digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate anxiety. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
